entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud: What’s New as of 2024-05-06?
entry.content:
Hello again SAP Community Members!

We're back this month to present you updates and new features for the SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud SM).

If you’d like to revisit all previous published blogposts about What’s New with SAP PaPM Cloud SM, you can use this tag for reference: https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/PaPMCloudSMWhatsNew/tg-p 

Modeling Screen

The latest update has removed the Recycle Bin from the Modeling screen. This adjustment aims to avoid confusion, as the Recycle Bin wasn’t designed to restore, but initially meant for storing deleted entities and functions.

Aside from the UI changes in the Modeling screen, a new enhancement is implemented. Previously, upon loading the environment data for the frontend, the system also included runtime artifacts. The runtime information for large environments is significant, as it includes the entire generated SQL code for procedures, annotation statements, and so on.

With the new enhancement implemented, the Modeling screen improves the initial loading time of the environment by not loading runtime artifacts especially for larger environments with a higher number of functions or fields. This allows us to reduce the generated SQL code for procedures, annotation statements, and so on.

System > History Monitor

The History Monitor screen released a new enhancement where you can retrieve an environment version overwritten by one of the historical versions (Snapshot version) chosen from the History Monitor.

When restoring a history version in the History Monitor by overwriting an existing environment, two (2) entries will be created and added in the History Monitor.

First, the system creates a history log with history type “DELETE” for the deletion of the overwritten. This history version captures the current state of the environment before the overwriting occurs.

In addition, it is also possible to restore the created history log.

This new feature also allows you to filter the History Monitor logs by time range. This way, you can easily extract the ones coming from a particular period.

(Example: Enter the time in the filter below, and the sample result based on the filtered ranged,)

Administration > Content Network

The Content Network screen has now been totally replaced by Content Management screen in SAP PaPM Cloud SM. This means that the previous Content Network screen can no longer be accessed from the hamburger menu or direct URL.

For more information, see SAP PaPM Cloud: Introducing the New Content Management Screen.

That concludes this month's What's New Blogpost! I am hoping to see you again in the next content! See you there!

entry.title: Re: Production Variance Analysis Report

Hi,

From manufacturing point of view, User can adopt the scope item-Analytics for Production Unit - Plan/Actual Production Cost (2QW) to Custom Analytical Queries and Query Browser apps leverage the Core Data Services (CDS) Views, which are annotated as consumption view.

You can optimize Plan-to-Produce across production units and optimize financial spend and manufacturing operations.

The production manager can compare between production cost of planning and execution, which covers all effort types plan costs, actual costs, target costs, control costs, and input/output variances.

entry.title: Re: WEBI regression testing and comparing Excel results
entry.content:
Is there a specific demand to compare using Excel?

If so, consider that there might be rounding issues regarding to the number format that gets exported to Excel, which, as i understand, also depends on universe settings (in case of that) or number conversions in Webi itself. So first thing to do is to check if the excel export itself contains the same values than you would expect in webi.

As you are in 4.2, you have more limitations than you would have in 4.3, like you cannot use OData to access the old BI platform or use Webi as a data source. So i see why you are trying to use excel. But when it comes to differences on that decimal level, it more looks like a data/number conversion issue when exporting the report as excel between the different SP releases, so i would then not export to excel, but to a plain text/csv so that there is no other application doing something with number formatting or conversions like excel could possibly do. Then, comparing text files can be done easily with lots of tools that offer some sort of automation and are free - like Notepad++ comparison or command line tools like "fc" in windows - linux even better tools available i guess -, also using regex to sort out those minimal differences. 

of course it depends on the number of reports you need to compare how much time it makes sense to spend in an automation comparison on that level.

entry.title: Re: New 1H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
@dba 
Hello David,

shift planning is a very wide area. It can span from an very light-weight planning of shifts of a team of 5 for the next week to ensure a 12hours presence in a shop till super-sophisticated planning process with skills and qualification integration, maybe even ergonomics aspect that a worker does not do for more than 2 hours same movements and so on.

So here are a couple of thoughts:

We enhance are team calendar so that an easy overview of a teams shifts and assigning different shifts to them is possible. This would be the lightweight solution.

All other we do not build in SF. We rather build upon integrations. We already have an integration to a super-sophisticated shift planning - our SAP Digital manufacturing. And there are hundreds of partner solutions out there each tailored for a specific industry that can also integrate to us.

On your second question: when is a working day regarded to be a working day? Please check my other release blogs where we introduced Cross midnight time recording and where I elaborated on this topic. You might need to search a bit, think it was 2021 or 2022.

The logical working day is the day on which the shift starts. So, in your example, if the employees planned working time is from 22:00 (10pm) Monday to 06:00 (am) Tuesday, then all recorded times count for Monday. If the employee wants to take this day off as vacation, he needs to record it with calendar date of Monday. So the principle is: shift start time defines the logical day. We also provide a nightshift bandwidht, however, this is not mandatory and has only relevance for the end time of the night shift. For example:

Shift is from 22:00 - 06:00. Employee works in this shift, all good. But then he takes a break of 1 hour at 06:05 and afterwards he starts working overtime from 07:00 - 09:00. Shall this 2 hours count to Monday or Tuesday (he started work on Monday). Here you can apply the night shift bandwidth. Set it to 22:00 - 10:00 for example and all recorded hours till 10:00 next day are regarded to belong logically to Tuesday. But note that this is only of relevance if the start time of a working time record on the next morning is AFTER the end time of planned time. If the start of the working time record is inside the planned working time, then it is always regarded to belong to the day on which the shift starts. 
I know, a bit complicated, that´s why the nightshift bandwidth is only for a small customer segment that might have lots of clock in / outs on a day, hence lots of time records, and one of them starts AFTER the end time of a night shift. With the nightshift bandwidth you can define another time period in which those time shall be regarded as belonging to the prior day.

Hope this helps

It is a night shift

entry.title: Get to know what’s new for SAP SuccessFactors Learning with 1H2024 release
entry.content:
Not surprisingly, AI is listed as the #1 trend in the recently published 2024 HR Trends: The Year of AI report, highlighting that all forms of AI to improve day-to-day productivity and employee engagement will be top of mind for organizations in 2024. Each year, the SAP SuccessFactors research team aggregates and synthesizes data from a wide range of carefully selected global and regional reputable business press resources that put forward HR trends and predictions and conduct a content analysis to derive key themes, or “meta-trends,” common among them to gain a comprehensive understanding of the most prevalent and important HR and workforce trends for the upcoming year. Here, AI dominates the 2024 trends discourse in terms of how it will transform the labor market, how it will drive productivity, and how it will need to be managed to prevent negative outcomes.

And trend #2 is skills and the fact that they are becoming the center of HR practices. The 2024 trends indicate that perhaps nowhere will the impact of AI have more important implications or be felt more strongly in the coming year for HR than in the areas of workforce and skills planning. Organizations will be forced to reconsider what AI skills they need, identify who (internally or externally) possesses those skills, and determine how they should best fill identified skills gaps in the short- and long-term – be it through reskilling and upskilling of existing talent, redeployment, external sourcing of full-time hires, or leveraging contract workers. Get valuable insights and advice about what to consider to get your workforce prepared for these upcoming changes in the blog Upskilling Your Workforce for the AI Era.

What does all of this mean in the area of learning? In past years, the focus of many Learning and Development organizations was on engaging learners with a modern experience, leveraging learning experience platforms (LXP). While offering an engaging learner experience is still relevant, this approach alone has not solved the challenge of increasing skills growth across the organization while aligning learning to strategic business goals. Learning and development (L&D) organizations are tasked to find the right balance between building up the strategic skills needed across their organization at an accelerated pace while helping employees to stay focused on the skills they need to stay relevant and prepared for the future. The good news is that employees are more than aware of this need and keen to receive training and other development opportunities to gain skills like in the context of AI. While all this transformation is going on, each and every organization needs to also keep up pace with ever changing rules and laws - even more driven by emerging technologies like the EU Artificial intelligence act as one prominent and most recent example. L&D organizations play a major role in keeping the workforce informed and compliant to minimize the risk for any damage claims or legal actions.

Both of these ‘trends’ highlighting the importance of AI and skills are highly reflected in our SAP SuccessFactors learning and talent product strategy. That is, to empower HR organizations to build a future-ready workforce using machine learning & AI-based talent intelligence, along with other unique product capabilities to upskill and reskill at scale and foster continuous talent development. The talent intelligence hub across SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including SAP SuccessFactors Learning, uses AI to continuously help organizations and employees understand their skills and match people with personalized career pathways.  

In addition, with the 2H2023 release we delivered a new and re-architected learning home page to help organizations build up skills needed by offering personalized and prioritized learnings for every employee based on the areas they "have to learn", "need to learn" and "want to learn”. You can find more information here: Exciting news for SAP SuccessFactors Learning in 2... - SAP Community.

With this 1H2024 release, we further enhanced the new integrated learning experience with features that support more learner interactions - for example with optional assignments and administrator recommendations, - giving more flexibility for administrators to brand and customize the experience with custom cards and banners on the new home page -, and with significant enhancements on other highly used pages such as search and the learning history page. Let’s deep dive into those capabilities a bit more.

Learners can now also directly access important learnings – those optional assignments or "need to learn" items - in the upper part of the new learning home page.  Here, learners find any non-required assignments they have. If allowed by the administrator, they can easily remove these assignments from their list, for example if they find that these are not currently relevant for them due to a change in their role, or because they already took a very similar training recently. This gives learners more freedom to self-manage their learning and saves the administrator the need to individually remove non-required training from learners' plans. Learners can also get reminder notifications for assignments without due dates, ensuring they stay informed and engaged, even when there is no mandatory completion deadline.

 In addition to AI-driven personalized recommendations which leverage the talent intelligence hub to surface recommendations based on the learner’s own learning history, skills in their growth portfolio, role in the company, and job role, - the new home page now also allows learners to access administrator recommendations along with peer suggestions under the “Suggested for me” section. This gives organizations more flexibility in surfacing just the right learning opportunities to specific learner groups, while providing users with the same easy, consistent way to interact with recommendations: bookmarking those they like, to save them for later, dismissing those they’re not interested in – all with an engaging visual experience.

Another highly anticipated feature for the new experience is now also available with the latest release: With the new custom card functionality, and a brand-new configurable banner, administrators can promote specific content, links and important updates to learners, directly on the home page. The new custom cards – which replace the custom tile experience on the legacy learning home page – can be used to navigate learners to other internal or external pages, be it to a 3rd party learning content provider or to prefiltered LMS catalog searches targeting specific audiences. For more content directly on the home page, admins can configure a card to surface a dialog on click. In the new dedicated admin tools for custom cards, administrators can create and manage the cards, determine who can see each card, during which time, where the custom card section should be on the page and in which sequence cards should appear.  

Similarly, to the custom cards on the SAP SuccessFactors home page, administrators can create learning cards that link directly to an internal deep link, such as a critical course, or external navigation pages, such as videos or targeted content libraries from any of our Open Content Library partners. Cards with dialog can support embedded iframes, HTML, images, videos, rich text editor to support links, messages, announcement, and other related use cases.

At the very top of the page, the newly configurable banner is the dedicated place for organizations to highlight time-sensitive information and resources that learners can use to discover, guide, and prioritize their learning. Administrators can choose to keep the default banner or create their own set of up to 5 banners, with custom images, title, subtitle, and a button that links to another page as needed.

 

The new banner functionality comes with a brand-new admin page to make it easy to create and manage the custom banners. Much like with the custom cards, administrators can also choose to show specific banners to all active users or a targeted audience, as well as specify the availability period.

As we keep enhancing the learner experience, we are making further improvements to other critical pages beyond the home page. The revamped learning history, now available to all users with the 1H 2024 release, focuses on ease of use and simplicity. This ensures that users can access all the essential information of their historic training records at a glance and take action towards the next step in their career development.

When it comes to discovering new training, the new search experience now has a filter for regions, to help employees quickly find courses based on their preferred locations. Note that it allows to select multiple regions, which can be useful for learners who frequently work at different locations.

Course home – where users find the details of a course – is now also available to users with external shopping accounts. Those users will now see dynamic actions like “register”, “buy course” or “add to cart” on the page, depending on the item classification and whether there are vouchers available. This means that these users now also get to benefit from the latest experience.

On the mobile side, the new learning experience now also supports optional assignments in Important Learning, self-directed items in Invest in Myself, and Task Observations in Requests for Me. Additionally, the Required Assignments cards now support engagement with Curricula requirements.

    

Finally, support for offline learning with downloaded content is now available on Android. It has the same usage flow as iOS, enabling users to advance their learning when they’re without a network connection. We also plan to integrate this feature within the Integrated Learning Experience in a future release.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

In addition, as we continue our journey towards a person-based learning model, it’s important to take the skills and knowledge a person gains, across their different employments, into account. With this in mind, we are introducing the bulk merge capability to help admins to tie multiple user accounts together, either from different employments or from the conversion of an external to an internal user in SAP SuccessFactors Learning. This brings a significant gain in time and efficiency for admins, who previously could only merge users individually. 

As this has been highly requested, I want to point out that we have delivered links to other Learning pages accessible directly from a drop-down menu at the top right of the integrated learning experience page with a separate patch in February of this year. Managers and supervisors can quickly access their "my team" page and any approvals. Learners can also easily access other quick links, any custom pages, and their learning history.

While more and more customers are adopting the new integrated learning experience, we are giving additional flexibility in how customers can roll out the change to their organization. Companies that have adopted the new integrated learning experience have the option to hide the user toggle to the legacy experience and only show the new experience to their users.  Those customers who need a bit more time can still opt-out of the integrated learning experience until the November 2024 release.

Are you interested in more information on the 1H2024 release highlights? Then check out the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2024 Release Highlights Learning video,  Release Highlights Brochure  Release Highlights Video and explore more Release Information at our customer community. You can also find a list of all enhancements on the What’s New Viewer and leverage the Roadmap Explorer to learn more about what’s planned for the future!

Talk to you soon!

Margit Bauer, Director Product Marketing for SAP SuccessFactors Learning

  2) EHP8 to S/4

Does any have some feedback on this matter ?

SAP Treasury and Risk Management FIN Treasury 

 

Stéphane

entry.title: How to maintain multiple payment advice email recipients by implementing custom logic？
entry.content:
Overview

To maintain multiple email recipients for payment advice, I basically need to do the following steps:

1. Maintain email address in business partner master data. When payment advice forms are created, the Output Parameter Determination app retrieves the email address entered in the master data of the business partner. 

2. Use the Addition of Email Recipients Business Add-In (BAdI) to implement custom logic that enables sending of payment advice outputs to multiple email addresses, including TO, CC and BCC recipients.

3. Maintain the email address defined in custom logic in the payment advice determination step for email recipient in the Output Parameter Determination app. The custom logic is called during output parameter determination to determine the required email recipients.

4. After a payment run, check if the output items have been sent to the specified recipients.

Procedure

Maintain Email Address in Business Partner Master Data

In the Manage Business Partner Master Data app, I maintain info@partner.com, bp_gen_email_1@sap.com, bp_gen_email_2@sap.com as email address in the address details of the supplier. info@partner.com is standard communication email while   bp_gen_email_1@sap.com and bp_gen_email_2@sap.com are additional email addresses.

Implement the BAdI and Create Custom Logic

  1. In the Custom Logic app, choose Create to create a new implementation.
  2. Search for the BAdI Addition of Email Recipients (ID: APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS) and select it as the extension point.

3. Add FFO_PAYM_ADV as filter value.

4. Enter a description and ID for the implementation.

5. Review the details of the implementation. If no change is required, create and publish it.

6. Open code editor to create custom logic. You can utilize the sample code provided as a basis or reference to create your own custom code.

*The following is sample code for enabling multiple TO email recipients. The email recipients are supposed to be retrieved from the ones that I have maintained in communication information in the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

 

DATA: ls_additional_email_recipient LIKE LINE OF additionalemailrecipients.

SELECT addressid
   FROM i_buspartaddress  WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
   WHERE businesspartner = @recipient
   INTO TABLE (lt_address_id).

IF lt_address_id IS NOT INITIAL.
  " Get Email Address
  SELECT emailaddress
  FROM i_addressemailaddress_2  WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
   FOR ALL ENTRIES IN _address_id
  WHERE addressid  = _address_id-addressid
  INTO TABLE (lt_emailaddress) .

  LOOP AT lt_emailaddress ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_emailaddress>).
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'TO'.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = <fs_emailaddress>-emailaddress.
    APPEND ls_additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.
  ENDLOOP.

ENDIF.

 

Maintain Email Recipients in BRF+ Settings

After defining custom logic for email recipients by using the BAdI implementation, I need to maintain the email address with email type code (TO, CC and BCC) in the business rules for payment advice email recipient.

1. In the Output Parameter Determination app, select Payment Advice from the Show Rules for dropdown list and Email Recipient for the Determination Step dropdown list.

2. Insert a new row in the decision table. Choose PAYM_ADV (Payment Advice) as output type, and select an email type code (for example, TO (To) for direct recipients).

3. In the Email Address field, choose Select Expressions from the dropdown list. In the dialog box that opens, apply the following search criteria and trigger the search:

  • Application Name=OPD_APOC_SYSTEM
  • Expression Type=Constant

4. In the search result, select the Add email recipients from custom logic constant and confirm.

5. Activate your changes.

During output parameter determination, the system will call the Addition of Email Recipients BAdI for each row that you have entered to determine additional email recipients.

Create Payment Run and Check Output Result

In the Manage Automatic Payments app, I create a payment run to pay the supplier. By calling the custom logic that I created for multiple TO email recipients, the payment advice for this payment run was sent to the three email recipients that I maintained in business partner master data.

Another Case

I want to payment advices to be sent to a regular recipient, and CC an additional recipient.

In the Manage Supplier Master Data app, I maintain bp_email_test@sap.com as Clerk's internet address at company code level (the regular recipient). 

And I hardcode the email address of the additional recipient in the custom logic.

*The following is sample code for enabling a TO email recipient and a CC email recipient. The TO email recipient is supposed to be retrieved from the information maintained in the clerk's internet address field (Clrk's internet add.) in the supplier master data at company code level, while the CC email recipient is hardcoded in the custom logic.

 

DATA: ls_additional_email_recipient LIKE LINE OF additionalemailrecipients.
DATA: ls_supplier TYPE i_suppliercompany.
DATA: ls_customer TYPE i_customercompany.

IF recipientrole = 'H'.    " Supplier
  SELECT SINGLE * INTO _supplier
                  FROM i_suppliercompany
                 WHERE supplier = @recipient AND
                       companycode = @outputcontrolapplicationobject+15(4).

  IF ls_supplier-accountingclerkinternetaddress IS NOT INITIAL.
    " Fill To email address from BP master data
    CLEAR ls_additional_email_recipient.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'TO'.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = ls_supplier-accountingclerkinternetaddress.
    APPEND ls_additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.

    " Fill the hardcode CC email address
    CLEAR ls_additional_email_recipient.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'CC'.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = 'hardcode_supplier_cc@sap.com'.
    APPEND ls_additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.
  ENDIF.
ELSEIF recipientrole = 'S'.    " Customer
  SELECT SINGLE * INTO _customer
                       FROM i_customercompany
                      WHERE customer = @recipient AND
                            companycode = @outputcontrolapplicationobject+15(4).

  IF ls_customer-accountingclerkinternetaddress IS NOT INITIAL.
    " Fill To email address from BP master data
    CLEAR ls_additional_email_recipient.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'TO'.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = ls_customer-accountingclerkinternetaddress.
    APPEND ls_additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.

    " Fill the hardcode CC email address
    CLEAR ls_additional_email_recipient.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailrole = 'CC'.
    ls_additional_email_recipient-outputrequestitememailaddress = 'hardcode_customer_cc@sap.com'.
    APPEND ls_additional_email_recipient TO additionalemailrecipients.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

 

I maintain the corresponding business rules in the Output Parameter Determination app as described in the first scenario. I create another payment run. By calling the custom logic that I created for TO and CC email recipients, the payment advice was sent to the desired recipients.

Conclusion

In this blog post, I described a step-by-step procedure about how to implement custom logic to enable sending of payment advices to multiple recipients, together with sample custom logics. I hope you find this useful when you have the same business cases. If you have any question about this topic, you're welcome to leave a comment!

For more information about payment advices, please check the following help documentation at SAP Help Portal:

For a brief understanding of outgoing payment advices, see Outgoing Payment Advices | SAP Help Portal

For common questions about payment advices, see Outgoing Payment Advices | SAP Help Portal

For step-by-step configuration procedures for payment advice outputs, see Configuring the Output of Payment Advice Forms: Email Channel | SAP Help Portal and Configuring the Output of Payment Advice Forms: Printout | SAP Help Portal

For supported extensibility options for payment advices, see Customizing Payment Advice Email Templates | SAP Help Portal and Adding Custom Fields to Payment Advice Forms | SAP Help Portal

For standard documentation about this topic, see Adding Additional Email Recipients for Payment Advices | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Shared Trouble is security doubled: the shared responsibility model for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
One of the advantages of cloud applications is that you don’t have to take care of security. Which is true – mostly. In reality, while most of the security responsibilities and tasks are taken over by the cloud service provider, there are some things which the customer still holds responsible for. And that also goes for the security responsibilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 Now, the title of this article specifically mentions SAP S/4HANA Cloud, without specifying whether it refers to private or public cloud. This is intentional as the focus is on the customer's responsibilities in the S/4HANA Cloud responsibility model – and in the following I’d like to highlight the responsibilities of the customer in general, while detailing the differences between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Privat Edition versus SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 Responsibilities in General

 In terms of security, responsibilities are relatively clearly split between the Cloud Service Provider (CSP) and the customer. On a high level, the CSP’s responsibility includes security operations, network application, database management, operating system management, and the bare metal, unless partnering with a hyperscaler.

 The customer, on the other hand, is responsible for the application's access and security. However, there's a significant distinction depending on whether we're discussing SAP S/4HANA public or private cloud.

 Looking at it from another perspective, as the cloud service provider, we don't have access to your customer data, user identities, authentication, and business processes. The responsibility for these aspects lies with the customer. Conversely, security for the application server, database, operating systems, physical security, and bare metal security falls under our, SAP's, jurisdiction as a cloud service provider.

Understanding the difference is crucial, not just for security, but for adjacent topics as well.

 Auditing cloud applications

 In order to get an overview, let’s begin with how auditing is approached in different cloud deployment options. This will help elucidate why it's critical to clearly distinguish responsibilities between you as a customer and SAP as the cloud service provider.

 When examining IT general controls, one should consider business processes, applications, and infrastructure. These elements require auditing by your auditor. IT application controls relate to business transactions and processes. The software, such as SAP S/4HANA, handles this. However, as the customer, you define the business processes and are therefore accountable to the auditor to ensure these processes function as designed.

 Secondly, there are application-related controls. These involve access management, change management, security configuration, and monitoring of application jobs and integration scenarios. The goal here is to ensure the application is implemented securely and correctly to meet the requirements of the business processes.

 The same principle applies to IT infrastructure, which must be implemented correctly to support business processes without negative impacts. This includes operating system and database security, but also physical data center security.

 Customer Audits

When considering various cloud service models, the scope of the auditor's role varies significantly between on-premise installations and software as a service. In an on-premise model, the customer’s auditor must assess everything from physical security to business process controls.

Different Cloud Deployment Models

In an Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) model, the auditor would need to rely on the cloud service provider for physical data center security and hardware matters. However, everything else, including service operating systems and databases, remains the customer's responsibility and must be audited accordingly.

 With Platform as a Service (PaaS), like S/4HANA private cloud, responsibilities for the security of operating systems and databases shift to the cloud service provider.

Software as a Service (SaaS) extends this concept further, in cases like the SAP S/4HANA public cloud, where standardized software is used by all customers.

 To manage these varying responsibilities, the SOC (Service Organization Control) report, specifically the SOC 2 report has been established as an industry standard. This report is an attestation from auditors that all controls regarding IT infrastructure and application responsibility are functioning as designed.

The SOC report's purpose is to prevent each customer's auditor from having to verify that a CSP is maintaining their controls correctly.

However, it's crucial to note that customers must request the SOC 2 report regularly from the CSP, review it, and ensure it aligns with their security requirements. Subsequently, the company and its IT auditors need to review the controls within the SOC 2 report to verify everything is in order.

The differences in SAP S/4HANA deployment models

Let's delve into the S/4HANA application, specifically focusing on the differences between private and public cloud options.  

To do this, we could proceed alphabetically by topic, but the most effective approach would be through the lens of the SAP Secure Operations Map. This tool, although developed before the advent of cloud technology, remains relevant as it comprehensively covers all tasks required for running applications like S/4HANA.

SAP Secure Operations Map

As a brief overview, the map starts at the top with the organization, which isn't directly related to IT. It emphasizes the need for security governance awareness and risk management - an area that's often overlooked. In essence, it's crucial to identify potential risks, prepare responses, and manage them appropriately.

Next, the secure operations map addresses the application itself, highlighting aspects like access management, authentication, authorizations, and custom code security. It considers all customizations made in your SAP S/4HANA system. 

On the system level, the map focuses on security hardening, secure SAP code, our secure development process, and security monitoring.

At the bottom of the map, mirroring the cloud responsibilities we've discussed, there’s the environment. This includes network security, operating system, database security, and client security.

Taking a closer look at this, and returning to the cloud deployment models we discussed earlier, we see that the Secure Operations Map's five topics (environment, system, application, process, and organization) are all the customer's responsibility in an on-premise environment.

In a private environment, the responsibility transitions between SAP and the customer around the application area. In a public environment, we, as the service provider, shoulder a larger share of the responsibility compared to the customer. 

Network security is a shared responsibility between customers and SAP, regardless of whether the environment is private or public. As a customer, you must ensure that the network you use to access applications is secure. For instance, in a private environment, you may use SAP GUI via a VPN tunnel to our network. In contrast, in the public cloud, you can only access the software through a web browser over the internet. In both cases, you as the customer need to ensure that the network on your end is secured.

The operating system database security is our responsibility, again, regardless of whether it's a private or public cloud. Client security, on the other hand, such as ensuring the SAP GUI or web browser is set up securely, falls under the customer's domain in both cases.

When it comes to system security hardening, it's our responsibility, fully. Secure SAP code, from a development perspective, is our responsibility in both private and public cloud scenarios. However, there's a key difference when it comes to patch management in the private cloud. Here, updates are individual per customer and may affect business processes or require system restarts. Therefore, we coordinate with you before initiating any updates, unlike in the public cloud, where updates affect all customers.

Security monitoring and forensics represent another shared responsibility, but there are differences between Rise and Grow. In Rise, due to access to most of the ABAP stack, similar to an on-premise environment, you receive more information, including infrastructure logs provided by our private cloud operations team, a service called LogServe.

In a public cloud scenario, we provide a limited number of logs for security monitoring. Some information, technically, is shared among all customers; hence, we can't share it with individual customers.

The application

Let's delve into the application, specifically User and Identity Management.

When we discuss roles and authorizations, we see a distinction between private and public cloud environments. In a private cloud, customers retain access to the PFCG, the SAP system transaction where roles and authorizations are defined and implemented. It's important to note that while you do have PFCG access in the private cloud, it's not usually full access. You typically can't assign authorizations or access the roles and authorizations definition, PFCG, as an admin, particularly when it comes to technical security topics.

In the public cloud, we implement a different strategy. We use what we call business catalogues, the smallest units of roles and authorizations. These catalogues are combined to create roles within the application, aligning with your business processes.

Regarding Custom Code Security, your code is generally your responsibility. However, there's a slight difference between private and public clouds. In the private cloud, you have more opportunities to program additional applications on top of S/4HANA. In contrast, in the public cloud, we provide Developer Extensibility that allows business application extensions but limits access to low level functionality.

The last main topic from within the Secure Operations Map is Processes and Organizations. This is primarily the customer's responsibility, which is why we won’t discuss it here.  

Conclusion: Lots and less to think about

As mentioned in the opening of this article: moving to the cloud gives you a lot less to think and worry about, especially when it comes to security. However, it does not relieve customers from all responsibility - something to keep in mind. On the other hand, we at SAP will support now and in the future by guiding customers through those settings they are responsible for. And by making S/4HANA Cloud the most secure ERP system.

entry.title: ACH/ WIRE RETURN Process in S/4 Hana
entry.content:
ACH/ WIRE RETURN Process in S/4 Hana

Problem Statement:

ACH or wire returns are received in Electronic Bank Statement for the paid invoices. The return process needs to be applied to the invoices and reset the payments.

Since returns are usually not received soon, the actual payments are already cleared/reconciled with bank statements.

To reissue the correct payments, the reversal original payment would not possible unless bank reconciliation postings are reverted which if done can open all payments received in that bank statement. To avoid such hassles, Account Payables team posts duplicate invoice.

Adverse Impact of the existing process:

  • Duplicate postings for one invoice
  • Account Payables & Bank Reconciliation issues.
  • Delays in Audit Reporting
  • Issues with 1099 reporting due to over tax exposure for supplier due duplicate invoice postings & payments.

The following document suggests the best way to manage ACH/Wire returns:

Instructions for the Treasury Team

Step1: Apply the ACH/Wire return received in Bank Statement using “Reprocess Bank Statement Items” Fiori App”

 

 

 

Step2: Select “Post to G/L Account” and then select “Add.”  Enter and Post ACH/wire return amount to Accounts Payable adjustment GL account viz. 2100XXX.

 

Step3: Select “Simulate” to ensure the accounting and posting date are correct and post.

Step4: Now, we need to find the original ACH/wire payment which cleared in SAP. Get the bank reconciliation clearing document from there and reverse the entire batch. The posting period should be the date of the ACH/wire return. This is needed to allow reversal of the payments.

 

Step5: Reprocess all the payments under the ACH payment batch except for the returned payment (note: this step is not necessary for wire payments). Select “Post to G/L Account” and debit same AP adjustment GL account 2100XXX for the returned ACH/wire payment amount.

Step6: Select “Simulate” to ensure the accounting and posting date are correct and post.

 

Step7: The Treasury Team will send an email to the Accounts Payable Team to show a payment was returned and provide the supporting information from the Bank.

 

Instructions for the AP Team

Step8: Confirm A/P invoice is open- Reset and Reverse the payment document.

  • Please note if the ACH/wire return occurred during A/P close, the A/P team will need to book the following reversing journal in GL SAP, until A/P opens in the following period. This Journal entry will auto reverse in first working day of the following month.

Dr ACH/Wire Clearing Account                               XXXX
Cr AP adjustment Account                                                                                        XXXX

  • AP team will reset and reverse in the first working day of the following month.

If ACH/WIRE return received on 28th of Feb-2024, AP will book a JE in SAP GL with posting date of 28th Feb-2024. This JE will be reversed on 1st March -2024. (First working day of the month). AP will reset and reverse the payment document with the with the posting date of 01st March-2024.

 

 

 

Original Payment for Returns

 

 

 Step9: Go to the “Reset cleared items” App window displaying the payment document to be reversed.

 

Step10: Select the “Reset and Reverse” option (use the date of the return for the posting date) in case in same month.

 

Step11: Now you can see payment reversal document.

 

Step12: Confirm reversed document. The original invoice is open to repayment and no duplicate invoice needs to be posted.

Once, reason of payment failure is resolved you can repay the supplier.

Please find below the accounting entries for the ACH /Wire return process

                                                            Debit                      Credit

                                                                                    

1) Initial Invoice processed in AP

 

Expense

  

    2,216.12

 
 

A/P

  

    -2,216.12

     

2) Initial Invoice paid in AP

  

A/P

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Bank Clearing Account

  

   - 2,216.12

     

3) Initial Payment Cleared (EBS File)

 

Bank Clearing Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Cash Account

  

    -2,216.12

     

4) ACH Return (EBS File)

  

Cash Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

AP Adjustment Account

  

    -2,216.12

     

5) Reverse Initial Payment Cleared (EBS File)

Cash Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Bank Clearing Account

  

    -2,216.12

     

AP Adjustment Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Cash Account

  

   - 2,216.12

     

7) AP Team Reverse Initial Payment

 

Bank Clearing Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

A/P

  

   - 2,216.12

     

 

 8.AP Team Reissues Payment

  

A/P

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Bank Clearing Account

  

   - 2,216.12

     

9) Reissued Payment Cleared (EBS File)

 

Bank Clearing Account

  

    2,216.12

 
 

Cash Account

  

   - 2,216.12

The ACH/Wire return process explained above is simple and easily adoptable. It helps us resolves all the adverse impacts of faulty process and ensures accurate reporting. Hope, this document equips you well to tackle his problem in future.

entry.title: Introducing SAP Performance and Profitability Management on Alibaba
entry.content:
Our newest hyper-scaler for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) ALIBABA, is here to further our commitment to provide exceptional service to our customers.

Yes, you have read it right! In this post, I'll walk you through how to subscribe SAP PaPM solutions via Alibaba Cloud so that you can optimize and transform your businesses.

Global Account & Sub-account on Alibaba
Subscribe PaPM Cloud from Sub-account
Create a role collection and add users
Start using PaPM Cloud

 

Global Account & Sub-account on Alibaba

To use SAP PaPM Cloud on Alibaba you need to have a Global Account & Sub-account on Alibaba.

Prerequisites : If you already have a Sub-account in Alibaba then add SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud | papm-service entitlement and select plan as default.

If you do not have a Global Account in Alibaba, you can start creating one by raising a SAP ticket. Then proceed with Sub-account creation from Alibaba BTP Cockpit.
Below is the list of inputs needed for the SAP ticket
a. Description : Request on-boarding of CN40 Data Workbench Saa
b. Component : XX-HST-CDC
c. Priority : Medium
d. System : SCP
e. Main impact : Globalization
f. Please fill out this Excel sheet , in the entitlement section add “SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud | papm-service” and select plan as default.
g. Attach the excel sheet to the ticket.

While selecting the region for the sub-account please select cf-cn40 to select Alibaba hyper-scaler.

Subscribe PaPM Cloud from Subaccount

Follow the steps below to subscribe to PaPM Cloud. For more detailed instructions, refer to the corresponding blog post SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud: Subscription & Decommission.

In the Alibaba sub-account you can find a “Create” button on your upper right corner.

After clicking on “Create” button you would get a pop up. Search for SAP PaPM in the Service section and select Plan as default, then click on “Create”.

It will take few moments to complete the subscription process.

Create a role collection and add users

For creation of role collection and addition of users please go through this blog post SAP PaPM Cloud: Basic Administration.

Start using PaPM Cloud

Go the the subscription page and click on SAP PaPM.

Authenticate yourself and now you are good to use SAP PaPM cloud.

entry.title: New Recruiting Offer Letter Template Rules - 1H 2024
entry.content:
The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2024 release introduces interesting enhancements to streamline the offer letter process. With the new "Configure Rule to Preselect Offer Letter Templates" feature, HR professionals can now automate template selection based on predefined criteria, optimizing efficiency and ensuring consistency. Additionally, the "Configure Business Rules to Add Conditional Sections in Offer Letters" empowers users to tailor offer letters dynamically, incorporating specific sections based on individual circumstances. These advancements signify a significant leap forward in personalized and efficient HR operations, promising enhanced user experience and streamlined processes.

 

Business Use Cases:

  1. This offer letter preselection functionality streamlines the offer process, ensuring that the appropriate offer letter template is selected automatically for recruiters, saving time and reducing errors.
  2.  The ability to add conditional sections in offer letters allows HR teams to personalize the content based on specific conditions such as the candidate's location, experience level, or role requirements.

Combined, these two business rule scenarios reduce the amount of time and effort Recruiting users spend in generating offer letters by selecting the template and letting them add tokens in place of writing paragraphs.

 

Pre-requisite to add Conditional Sections in Offer Letters:

1. Ensure that you have permission Manage MDF Recruiting permissions> ConditionalOfferLetterToken.

2. Once you have this permission, you can go to Manage Data and search for the option called ConditionalOfferLetterToken. Clicking Take Action, and Make Correction allows you to change the label for a token to assist users in knowing what each token will contain. The labels can be translated. (Be informed that there are only 100 tokens available for the administrator to configure)

3. Add the created token(s) on the respective offer letter template:

 

Configuration:

Now, let's see rule scenarios that are located in Recruiting, in Admin Center >Configure Business Rules when you select + (Create New Rule)

The next step will be for you to create the rules within Business Rules Engine for both these scenarios. Rules can be as simple as adding the text directly into the rule, or very complex, including the use of lookup tables. Using extended functionality makes it possible to add formatting to offers, including images. You are also able to add HTML formatting like <b>, <br> or <p>.

Preselect Offer Letter Template Rule Sample: My if condition is for a specific offer letter template to be preselected, if the candidate's country of residence is "United Stated".

Add Conditional Sections in Offer Letters Rule Sample: I have used "IF" and "Else IF" conditions for the tokens to populate different verbiage on the same offer letter based on the job's employment type.

The next step will be for you to assign a new rule, go to Manage Rules in Recruiting Job Application. Choosing a template from the Job Requisition Template dropdown list expands the Template-level Rules section to display Rule for Offer Letter Template Selection. Click on Add a Rule below it to select and assign the rule scenario to the rule.

At this point in time, you have completed all configurations necessary. You should now be able to go ahead and test your configuration.

Based on the configuration, when the recruiters go to select the offer letter for the candidate, the appropriate offer letter will be preselected. The recruiter still has the flexibility to select other offer letters from the list available in the section as far as they have permissions to it. The preselection will reduce letter selection and error in selection.

When the recruiter previews or clicks on next step from there, based on the Add Conditional Sections in Offer Letters rule, if the rule conditions are met, (from the above example) only one of the tokens will resolve into a paragraph and the other token will disappear & will not resolve.

In conclusion, by implementing these configurations, recruiters can streamline their workflow and reduce the time spent on repetitive tasks, allowing them to focus on more strategic aspects of recruitment. 

If there are any other creative cases that you have configured for yourself/ your clients, feel free to update in the chat. Hope this information was useful and thanks for taking the time read this.

entry.title: Spice Up Your Email alerts with Personalized Images: A Step-by-Step Guide Using SuccessFactors EC
entry.content:
In today's digital world, emails are our go-to for staying in touch with colleagues and clients. But how can we make sure our messages stand out? One way is by adding pictures! It's easier than you think, using SuccessFactors Employee Central. Here's a simple guide to jazz up your emails with personalized images.

You can use this method for any HTML-based content, such as alerts notifications or welcome email to the new users.

Step 1: Store Your Image Safely on the Cloud

Start by picking a safe place to keep your image online. you can also upload the image to an external secured host.

Step 2: Get a Shareable Link for Your Image

Once your picture is uploaded, get a link that anyone can use to see it. It's like having a special key to open the door to your image.

the link would look like the following: "www.example.com/image.JPG"

 

Step 3: Drop the Image Link into Your Email

Now comes the fun part! Use a little bit of code to drop your image into your email. go to your email template you wish to customize, at the proper part of the email content, paste the code, It's as easy as copying and pasting. Here's an example:

HTML:

 

 

<img src="www.example.com/image.JPG" alt="Your_Image_Description">

 

 

Just replace "www.example.com/image.JPG" with the actual link you got earlier. And don't forget to give a short description of your image so everyone knows what it's about!

 

Step 4: Give it a Test Run

Before you hit send, make sure everything looks good. Send yourself a test email to see how it appears. This way, you can fix any issues before sending it out for real. it will look like the following: 

 

 

 

And that's it! With SuccessFactors Employee Central, adding personalized images to your emails is a breeze. So go ahead, give your messages that extra pop and leave a lasting impression on your recipients!

entry.title: Re: How to disable Auto-fill function of SAP in MM Materi...
entry.content: @Dominik Yes, your understanding is correct. However, the material number field are populated by the number that i enter previously for a different Tcode. (eg. I key in the material number for MMO3 then when the number are populated by the same material number when i am at MM01)

entry.title: Identifying future dated transactions during a termination event in Employee Central
entry.content:
Business Scenario

The expectation is that, throughout an employee's life cycle, rehiring will be the sole authorized event after termination. This implies that if termination occurs prior to a promotion or data modification, it will result in complications when integrating with the payroll system.

Currently, it is required to manually review the employee's Job Information to determine if any future dated records have been produced and to implement the necessary adjustments or deletions to finalize the action in the event of termination.

Requirements from multiple customers are as follows:

  • In case there are any transactions created for a future date, upon entering a termination in EC, a message should be shown, asking the user to take necessary action and clean them up before the termination.
  • A notification or message indicating records with future dated transactions during imports would be important as well. This feature would be beneficial for both termination procedures and regular imports of effective dated records.

Solution Overview

To fulfill this requirement, one possible approach is to create a termination rule that makes sure that there are no future dated records in Job Info regarding the termination date. Understanding how termination action is handled in Employee Central is key to accomplishing this.

  • Termination is an event that uses the Employment Details as starting point and generates a record in the Job Info element.
  • The Job Info start date with the Termination event is set to begin one day after the termination date specified in the termination details. This procedure aligns with the standard practices of on premise SAP HCM, and ECP systems.
  • Every time a new record is created in Job Info, an end date is automatically assigned internally. If there are no future records, the end date is set as 31.12.9999. However, if there is a future record, the end date is determined as one day before the start date of the future record.

Configuration Details

Considering the system behavior mentioned before, we can create an OnSave rule within employment details to determine whether the termination record being generated in Job Info contains an end date of 31.12.9999 (indicating no future records) or if it does not match 31.12.9999 (indicating future records).

By implementing this simple rule, we can ensure that the new termination record is validated against any potential future records in the Job Info section.

Now, let's examine how this solution behaves in various scenarios:

Scenario 1: Termination with no future records in Job Info.

For this scenario we have an employee with only a hire record (01.01.2023), if we add a termination record on 04.01.2023 the end date for this new termination record will be 12.31.9999.

In this case the condition is not met (no future records) and no error message will be triggered.

Scenario 2: Termination with future records.

For this scenario we have an employee with two records, a hire record (01.01.2023) and a promotion (02.01.2024), if we add a termination record on 04.01.2023 the end date for this new termination record will be 01.31.2024, one day before the start date of the future promotion record.

In this case the condition is met (there are future records) and the error message will be triggered.

Scenario 3: Termination posterior to future records.

For this scenario we have an employee with two records, a hire record (01.01.2023) and a promotion (04.01.2023), if we add a termination record on 02.01.2024 the end date for this new termination record will be 12.31.9999.

In this case the condition is not met (no future records) and no error message will be triggered.

Additional notes:

 

  • If you have a requirement to have this rule working on imports as well, please validate that the user executing the imports has assigned permissions to trigger rules on import. More details Configuring Business Rules for Data Imports
  • After termination you can update other available fields in Employment Details without any error messages related to this rule.
  • Since 2H 2021, creating a termination record from Job History is not possible. That's the reason why this rule is triggered from Employment Details. More details 3103944 - Prevention of Special Record Creation in Job History ECT-130989 - 2H 2021

Conclusions

Meeting specific requirements often leads to deep analysis of customer needs and good understanding of SuccessFactors solution behavior as well as the available tools, such as business rules. Sometimes, complex requirements can be addressed with simple solutions.

entry.title: HCM Brasil: eSocial – Visualizador de Eventos Totalizadores de empregador (S-5013)
entry.content:
Olá pessoal, 

Dando continuidade às entregas do programa eSocial: Visualizador de Eventos Totalizadores de empregador (Transação PC00_M37_EFD_ER_SUMM), gostaríamos de comunicar que concluímos a entrega do evento totalizador S-5013 - Informações do FGTS consolidadas por contribuinte.

Desta forma, informamos que a SAP concluiu a entrega dos 3 eventos totalizadores de empregador do eSocial, para verificar os demais totalizadores de empregador, acesse os links abaixo:

  • HCM Brasil: eSocial – Novo programa Visualizador de Eventos Totalizadores de empregador (S-5011)
  • HCM Brasil: eSocial – Evento S-5012 adicionado ao Visualizador de Eventos Totalizadores de empregado

Atualize seu sistema com as seguintes SAP Notes abaixo: 

  • 3457666 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3457667
  • 3457667 - eSocial - Summarizing Events Viewer - S-5013

Após a instalação das SAP Notes, o evento S-5013 vai estar habilitado para seleção no campo Tipo de evento: 

Executando o programa, você irá visualizar as informações disponibilizadas nos eventos S-5013 com a distinção do log em apuração mensal e apuração Diária, seguindo o mesmo layout do evento: 

Lembrando que as informações disponíveis na visualização do log também podem ser exportadas para um arquivo CSV, conforme a opção Exportar arquivo na seleção do programa. 

Você também tem acesso ao  XML de retorno dos lotes através do programa eSocial: Monitor de lotes. 

A lista completa de entregas do eSocial do ano de 2024 está disponibilizada na SAP Note  3418378 - eSocial - Overview – 2024. 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. 

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço,
Guilherme Zenzen

entry.title: 1H 2024 the “Employee Experience” release
entry.content:
By now, you’re probably diving deep into the SAP SuccessFactors’ 1H 2024 release and you might have noticed that our attention is focused on none other than the employee. After all, we’re all an employee of one sort or another. We might be an individual contributor, a manager, a project lead, a department leader, an HR professional, or even a CEO. So, as we continue to invest in delivering the best user experience, we are happy to share what’s in store for every one of our customers’ employees, including you.  

Everyone has a story to tell and so does your organization

People are the most important element in an organization’s success. Understanding who your co-workers are, what your employees bring to the team, and easily connecting individuals to one another is important for every organization. That’s why I’m pleased to highlight the new People Profile experience. With the 1H 2024 release, you’ll experience the new profile “Spotlight”. It focuses on enabling employees to easily share their strengths and value throughout the organization. With a focus on progressive discovery, the profile Spotlight facilitates deeper exploration, supported by the new Profile Preview that’s present across the solution. The addition, the new Profile Preview enhances contextual understanding by providing essential information about each employee, fostering better connections and collaboration. With a layout and card design optimized for rapid engagement and comprehension, the new People Profile experience not only elevates the employee experience but also strengthens organizational cohesion and knowledge sharing. Best of all, the new Profile Preview and Spotlight are coming to our iOS and Android apps, too.

In addition, our new Org Chart makes it easy for employees to explore key aspects of their organization. Seamlessly connecting employees to the new Spotlight feature, the new Org Chart helps individuals to understand at-a-glance, everyone’s place within the organization. A clean and crisp visual experience makes navigating the organizational structure and hierarchy super intuitive. Plus, the lightning-fast UI means employees can delve deeper to explore more in less time.

AI provides every employee a competitive advantage

Joule is a game-changer when it comes to helping employees be as productive as possible. With a host of self-service capabilities for employees and managers, Joule empowers employees to effortlessly do more. The copilot’s clean and clear visual language makes it easy to focus on the task at hand. It’s conversational design, fuzzy matching for less-than-perfect spellers, and 1-tap suggestive answers, make everyday tasks as quick and seamless as possible. Moreover, Joule extends across all SAP solutions so the experience of using it is consistent and familiar. As we look ahead, by leveraging LLM and and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), questions like “Is emergency room service covered in the medical plan?” or “We are about to announce quarterly results, can I sell company stock?”, will be answered and include links to source documentation building confidence and trust with your employees.

With our new AI-assisted Goal creation experience, we’re helping employees and managers to craft well-defined, actionable, and measurable goals. Powered by generative AI, this new feature swiftly transforms ideas into comprehensive goals ensuring alignment with the organization's strategy. The benefit is a significant reduction in the time required to create meaningful goals. Plus, pre-prompting promotes ease of use and encourages adoption. The solution’s simple yet effective results make achieving goals more tangible, while motivating employees to strive for success. In addition, AI-assisted writing functionality is incorporated in the Goals experience to enable further employee-led refinement of goals.

Our new AI-assisted writing tool elevates communication. Whether you're grappling with finding the right words or seeking to enhance the tone of your communication, AI is your trusted companion. The comprehensive editing functionality offers suggestive approaches to getting your message just right. The solution even enables contextualized editing. For example, this includes prompts to "make it actionable" when giving feedback and other forms of purpose-built support tailored to specific tasks. Looking ahead, the text analyzer feature promotes inclusivity by guiding users toward more inclusive word choices.

With this release we’ve also enhanced the Applicant Workbench with the introduction of a skills-based intelligent recruiting experience that redefines talent acquisition. Recognizing that skills are the currency of a modern economy and the foundation of talent decisions, this platform offers insightful and expansive consideration of every candidate and their skills, enabling recruiters to make better informed decisions. The skills layout accelerates the time to review candidates by prioritizing skill matches and even adjacent skills. Furthermore, AI explanations frame the results, so recruiters understand the sourcing methodology. This enhances transparency and trust in the recruitment process.

A new benefits enrollment experience is here

With this release I’m excited to share the introduction of a new Benefits Enrollment experience. Embracing a consumer-style shopping approach, this solution delivers a familiar and intuitive experience and enables employees to navigate the benefits selection process with ease. With step-by-step guidance, employees can make informed choices with the help of embedded content that provides valuable insight into benefits options. A running expense total enables employees to manage their spend as they progress through the enrollment process, ensuring transparency and control over their benefits selections. With this new Benefits Enrollment experience, employees gain the tools and support to make decisions that align with their needs and preferences.

Engage in the flow of work with SAP SuccessFactors for Microsoft Teams

The updated app is a groundbreaking new experience for SAP SuccessFactors within the Microsoft Teams application, designed to seamlessly bring HR directly into the flow of work. Anchored by a new homepage, this experience serves as a central hub, making navigation effortless. With today's collaboration tools enabling employees to complete HR related tasks within the flow of work, employees have even more opportunities to self-serve. For example, they can give and receive feedback in the moment, and stay on top of timely activities such as spot award approvals and learning requirements. This not only enhances employee engagement but also helps ensure that HR tasks are seamlessly managed as part of employees' daily routines. The clean and simple design makes discovery of tasks and to-do’s quick and easy.

The 1H 2024 release: something for everyone

By placing the employee at the center, this release empowers individuals across all levels of the organization, recognizing that everyone has a story to tell. From the redesigned People Profile and Org Chart, which facilitate deeper connections and collaboration, to Joule and the AI-assisted writing tool, which streamline processes and enhance productivity, each innovation improves engagement and efficiency. Furthermore, the integration of SAP SuccessFactors with Microsoft Teams and the revamped Benefits Enrollment experience demonstrate a commitment to meeting employees where they are and simplifying how they accomplish HR related tasks. As we continue to invest in enhancing the employee experience, we are not only fostering a more connected and engaged workforce but also driving organizational success and growth.
